div1.entryFree:
Judicious, 1) pertaining to a judge or connoisseur: examined my parts with most j. oeillades, Wiv. I, 3, 68.
2) discerning, wise: Mcb. IV, 2, 16. Hml. III, 2, 29. Lr. III, 4, 76.
3) judicial, as practised in the distribution of public justice: his last offences shall have j. hearing, Cor. V, 6, 128.
